sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 20-year-old female being seen for her yearly follow-up for asthma, allergic rhinitis, and food allergy.
She was switched from Symbicort to Wixela due to insurance changes in April 2020.
She uses Zyrtec in the spring, summer, and fall for seasonal allergy symptoms, which tend to work well.
She has a known peanut allergy but tolerates almonds and cashews.
The patient has been taking Wixela 250/50 dose once in the morning and once at night, 3-4 days a week, and 1 puff per day on other days.
She has not had any urgent care or ER visits for breathing issues in the past year and has not been on prednisone for breathing issues.
Past medical history: The patient has asthma, allergic rhinitis, and a food allergy to peanuts.
She has been using Wixela and Zyrtec as prescribed.
Review of systems: The patient has been experiencing asthma symptoms, but they have been manageable with her current treatment regimen.
Current medications:
Wixela 250/50 dose (morning and night, 3-4 days a week)
Zyrtec (as needed, spring, summer, and fall)
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.

Physical exam: The patient's ears had some wax buildup, but the lungs sounded terrific, and the breathing test showed results similar to those from 2 years ago, which were better than the results from 2019 due to her current health status.
Test Results: The breathing test results from today were compared to the results from 2 years ago and 2019, showing an improvement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's asthma symptoms are currently manageable, and her breathing test results show improvement.
Problem list: Asthma; Allergic rhinitis; Food allergy to peanuts; Asthma (suspected); Allergic rhinitis (suspected); Food allergy to peanuts (suspected).
Plan:
I prescribed Alvesco 80 mcg (2 puffs twice a day) for asthma.
I will have the patient return in 4 months for a repeat breathing test and spirometry.
I will also refill the patient's Albuterol and EpiPen.
The patient will continue to use Zyrtec as needed for seasonal allergy symptoms.
The patient will continue to avoid peanuts and tree nuts.
The patient will also continue to use Wixela as needed, but will try to minimize its use to only when necessary.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 4 months for a repeat breathing test and spirometry, and the EpiPen will be refilled.
